what_it_does: The application-shell stylesheet for 'ConceptV Studio — design system manager'. Defines the three-column app grid (.dsa-shell: 248px side-nav / main canvas / 360px chat rail), and styles for every screen region: side nav + brand + collapsible drawer, canvas header, buttons, cards/sections/panels, the overview stat grid + activity feed + recent-canvases tiles, the inbox/drop zone, the icon canvas (search pill, category bar, icon grid, generation bottom-sheet), the colors canvas (palette grid + swatches), the chat rail (messages, suggested-action pills, composer), the Vi diamond mark, an empty/stub canvas, toasts, and a set of reusable 'inspo' helper classes.
observations: Every color/space/radius/shadow/font value is a var(--token); no raw hex except inside repeating-linear-gradient hatch patterns (rgba(224,192,16,...)) and a few inset shadow tints (rgba(31,26,18,...)).
Heavy recurring visual motif: 135deg repeating-linear-gradient gold hatch backgrounds appear in ~6 places (system-status, canvas-tile preview, drop zone, gen-panel, stub, hatch-band helper).
Dashed gold rules (1px dashed var(--accent-gold-soft)) are a consistent separator motif across headers, sections, composer, chat head.
Two keyframe animations defined inline: dsa-pulse (nav new-dot glow) and dsa-pulse-dot / dsa-pulse-dot used by inbox-thinking and chat.
Uses modern CSS: color-mix(in oklch, ...) for hover blends and tinted backgrounds in several rules.
Class namespace is almost entirely 'dsa-' (design-system-app) with a separate 'cv-' helper family and a 'vi-shape' mark.

Lines 764-773 are orphaned CSS declarations (padding/font/box-shadow/opacity...) sitting OUTSIDE any selector block, immediately after .cv-gold-pill closes at line 763 — they look like a leftover fragment of a toast-style rule whose selector was deleted.
The .dsa-toast.show and .dsa-toast .v rules are defined twice (lines 737-738 and again 772-773).
.dsa-icon-tile.generated::after sets content 'New' with a comment 'will be positioned by parent' but no positioning — effectively inert.
Body sets overflow:hidden and height:100% so the app is a fixed full-viewport shell with internal scroll regions.
standouts: Token-disciplined: the whole shell is painted from var() tokens, matching the project's single-source-of-truth law — the literal hatch rgba values are the visible exception.
A distinctive editorial/'inspo' visual language — bronze italic display eyebrows, dashed-gold rules, gold hatch bands — codified as reusable cv-* helpers.
conventions: Follows the no-raw-value rule via var(--token) almost everywhere (consumes the token registry rather than redefining it).
Consistent 'dsa-' BEM-ish region naming and 140ms var(--ease-out) transition timing throughout.
Breaks its own discipline with: literal rgba in hatch gradients, and orphaned/duplicate declarations at EOF (lines 764-773).
concerns: Lines 764-773: declarations outside any selector — invalid/dangling CSS the browser will ignore, and a duplicate of the .dsa-toast.show / .dsa-toast .v rules; reads as a botched edit leaving a fragment of a removed selector.
.dsa-icon-tile.generated::after has content but no positioning and the wrap uses .new-badge instead — dead rule.
Repeated literal rgba(224,192,16,...) hatch values across many rules are an un-tokenised recurring literal (a candidate token per the project's hardcoded-literal finder).
notes: The full app-shell CSS for ConceptV Studio's three-pane manager — token-driven and stylistically distinctive, but with a dangling/duplicated CSS fragment at the end (lines 764-773) that should be cleaned.
questions: Are the repeating gold-hatch rgba(224,192,16,...) values meant to become a token/helper, or intentionally left literal as a texture?
What selector did the orphaned declarations at lines 764-773 originally belong to (a removed toast variant)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header names it the design-system-manager app stylesheet and it styles exactly that app's shell and screens; the only mismatch is internal hygiene (the dangling EOF fragment).
apparent_themes: Token-as-truth consumption (paint from var(), don't redefine)
A codified editorial/inspo visual identity (bronze italics, dashed gold, hatch)
Full-viewport three-pane workspace shell with internal scroll regions
intention_signals: Trying to dress the design-system-manager app in a single, distinctive ConceptV visual language driven entirely from the token registry, so the manager itself demonstrates the design-system it manages, with reusable helper classes capturing the recurring inspo motifs.
novelty: The strong, specific aesthetic vocabulary expressed as reusable helpers (cv-bronze-eyebrow, cv-dashed-rule, cv-hatch-band, cv-gold-pill) and oklch color-mix hover blends give it a recognizable editorial character beyond a generic admin shell.
contribution: Contributes the complete visual/layout language of the ConceptV Studio manager — the three-pane shell grid, the dsa-* region styles for every screen, and the cv-* editorial helper set (eyebrows, dashed rules, hatch bands, gold pills) — all consuming the token registry.
summary_for_embedding: The application-shell stylesheet for ConceptV Studio's design-system manager: a three-column dsa-shell grid with token-driven styles for nav, canvas, cards, overview, inbox, icon/colors canvases, chat rail, toasts, and reusable cv-* editorial helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To give the design-system manager a single distinctive ConceptV visual identity painted entirely from the token registry, so the tool exemplifies the system it manages.
